Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Strong value for nutritional enhancement and functional food development because overexpression increased tomato fruit peel flavonols, especially rutin/quercetin glycosides, without gross phenotype penalty and with retention after paste processing.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes naringenin chalcone to form naringenin.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: No external cofactor reported; CHI generally functions without a redox cofactor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Petunia chi overexpression in tomato depleted naringenin chalcone and increased peel flavonols up to 78-fold, mainly rutin/quercetin glycosides, with smaller increases in kaempferol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in tomato, HPLC metabolite profiling of hydrolyzed and nonhydrolyzed peel extracts, Northern blot detection of chi transcripts, segregation analysis across generations, and field-trial validation.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Tomato fruit peel during ripening, especially breaker, turning, red, and overripe stages; strongest interpretation tied to turning/red peel where naringenin chalcone normally accumulates.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Established gene identity from Petunia chi cDNA; in vivo overexpression caused depletion of substrate-like naringenin chalcone and increased downstream flavonol glycosides, consistent with CHI activity.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: Developmental accumulation of naringenin chalcone in control peel together with undetectable endogenous chi transcript suggested this step as bottleneck; transgenic chi expression reduced naringenin chalcone and increased rutin/isoquercitrin/kaempferol glycosides.
Pathway_Genes.Favorable_Allele: Petunia hybrida chi transgene driven by double CaMV 35S promoter (pBBC50).
Pathway_Genes.Field_Trials: Yes. Homozygous transgenic plants grown in controlled open field trial conditions (1999 season, Stockton, California) maintained elevated whole-fruit flavonols; best lines approached yellow onion flavonol levels.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate-gene metabolic engineering based on pathway knowledge; Petunia chi cDNA amplified from plasmid pMIP41 and overexpressed in tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Independent transgenic tomato lines; single-insert lines followed over subsequent selfed generations including homozygous and hemizygous populations through at least the fourth generation.
Pathway_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive transgene expression under double CaMV 35S promoter; greenhouse growth and open field trial evaluation; paste processing also evaluated for product retention.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: HPLC with photodiode array detection on hydrolyzed and nonhydrolyzed extracts.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Authors note CHI may be the sole rate-limiting step in peel flavonol formation or may additionally stabilize/enhance other pathway enzymes/complexes. Significant isoquercitrin accumulation in high-expressing lines suggested downstream rhamnosyltransferase limitation. No increase detected in flesh or leaves despite transgene transcripts in some tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA gel blot (Northern) expression analysis; no public accession numbers reported.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: No gross vegetative phenotype differences; slight dullness in ripe red color likely due to reduced yellow naringenin chalcone accumulation; lycopene concentration unchanged; taste/flavor of paste unchanged.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General flavonoid core leading into flavonol branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible altered fruit color tone; possible downstream glycosylation bottleneck causing isoquercitrin accumulation; otherwise no major penalties reported.
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: Up to 78-fold increase in peel flavonols; hydrolyzed peel extracts reached up to 16.52 mg/g dry weight quercetin and 2.05 mg/g dry weight kaempferol, representing 66-fold and 57-fold increases over control, respectively; paste retained 65% of fresh-fruit flavonols and reached up to 1.9 mg/g dry weight paste (21-fold over control).
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a likely rate-limiting or key control step in tomato peel flavonol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Rutin / quercetin glycosides and kaempferol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Target_Product_Level_or_Property: Accumulation/content in fruit peel, whole fruit, and processed paste
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Primarily fruit peel; also whole fruit in field trials and paste after processing; flesh and leaves assayed as comparison tissues.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis; entry/early steps supplying flavonol branch
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Contextual pathway gene; relevant as an expressed upstream biosynthetic marker in peel but not directly manipulated for trait improvement in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with three malonyl-CoA units to produce a chalcone, the precursor of naringenin chalcone/flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Requires CoA thioester substrate chemistry; no special cofactor reported in paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript detectable in peel across fruit development with peak around breaker/turning stages, indicating upstream precursor-supply capacity for peel flavonoid synthesis; no direct genetic perturbation reported.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot expression analysis in peel, flesh, and leaves using heterologous Petunia chs-a probe.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing tomato fruit peel during green, breaker, turning, and red stages; also assessed in flesh and young leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Gene assignment based on known flavonoid biosynthetic enzyme identity detected with Petunia chs-a probe; no enzyme assay in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: chs transcripts were detectable in peel at all stages and peaked before/around high flavonol accumulation, consistent with an upstream role preceding CHI and FLS.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate pathway gene monitored by expression analysis with heterologous probe.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Tomato fruit tissues and leaves from cultivar FM6203; control and transgenic context for expression comparisons.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirectly linked through HPLC flavonoid profiling and Northern blot expression analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Expression was absent from flesh, matching negligible flesh flavonoid accumulation. The paper does not demonstrate causality of CHS variation for flavonol enhancement, only pathway competence.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA gel blot (Northern) expression analysis; no accession numbers reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Chalcone intermediate (leading to naringenin chalcone/flavonoid backbone)
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No gene-specific perturbation quantified; transcript present in peel at all stages, peaking during breaker and turning.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Rutin / quercetin glycosides and related peel flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit peel primarily; flesh and leaves for expression comparison.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Contextual pathway gene marking terminal flavonol formation capacity; useful for pathway interpretation but not directly engineered in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols such as quercetin and kaempferol through desaturation.
Pathway_Genes.Cofactor_or_Coenzyme_Requirement: Typically requires Fe2+, 2-oxoglutarate, and oxygen as a 2-oxoglutarate-dependent dioxygenase, though not explicitly tested here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript detectable in peel throughout development with peak at breaker/turning stages, supporting capacity for flavonol synthesis in peel; no direct perturbation tested.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Northern blot expression analysis with heterologous Petunia fls probe, interpreted alongside HPLC flavonol profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Developmental_or_Physiological_Context: Developing tomato fruit peel across ripening stages; also tested in flesh and leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Enzymatic_Function: Known pathway enzyme identity inferred from Petunia fls probe; no direct catalytic assay in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Evidence_for_Pathway_Position: fls transcripts in peel coincided with flavonol accumulation and fit its known downstream position after CHI-mediated formation of flavanones and subsequent hydroxylation/reduction steps.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Candidate pathway gene assessed by expression analysis using heterologous probe.
Pathway_Genes.Genetic_Population: Tomato fruit tissues and leaves from cultivar FM6203; analyzed in developmental series.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolite_Assay_Method: Indirectly connected through HPLC flavonol profiling and Northern blot expression analysis.
Pathway_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: The study did not manipulate fls, so its contribution is inferential. Transcript was undetectable in flesh, consistent with minimal flesh flavonoids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA gel blot (Northern) expression analysis; no accession numbers reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Flavonols including quercetin and kaempferol backbones
Pathway_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: No gene-specific perturbation quantified; transcript present in peel at all stages with peak during breaker and turning.
Pathway_Genes.Target_Metabolite_Product: Quercetin and kaempferol glycosides (e.g., rutin, kaempferol rutinoside)
Pathway_Genes.Tissue_or_Organ_of_Metabolite_Assessment: Fruit peel primarily; flesh and leaves for expression comparison.

Title: Overexpression of petunia chalcone isomerase in tomato results in fruit containing increased levels of flavonols
